title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Detective Superintendent Huishe informs Dr. Arthur Calgary that Mickey has been taken into custody and is expected to be charged within 24 hours. While Mickey claims to have been in the garden, Huishe suggests he could have used a large magnolia tree to climb into Darnton's room to commit the murder. Huishe also reports that Tina, who is recovering, has been making incoherent statements, including naming "Mickey" and uttering cryptic phrases such as "The cup is empty" and "The pigeon is on the mast." Calgary remains unconvinced of Mickey's guilt, believing the crime was instead a crime of passion.

At dusk, Calgary returns to Sunset Point to resolve the chaos he believes his arrival initiated. Upon arrival, he encounters Hester, Cressida Lindstrom, and a hostile Kirsten Lindstrom, who blames Calgary for the deaths of Philip Darwent and Tina. Calgary eventually gathers Leo Aigle, Gwendolyn Vaughan, and Mary Darland in the study to address the group. 

Calgary explains his motive is to prevent the "disaster of the innocent" caused by his presence. He observes that when he first arrived with news to clear Jack's name, the residents seemed satisfied with Jack's guilt because it provided a convenient explanation for the murder of Mrs. Aigle. He notes that while others justified Jack's actions through his mental state or misfortune, Kirsten Lindstrom was the only person who openly condemned him as "immoral."
